content.text: Metrics work (200). Now I'll update the Prometheus config to scrape the 3 RabbitMQ pods via their stable headless DNS names (no RBAC/SD needed).

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-maximal-lifecycle-roll-af2b323e-cluster-a
cat > /tmp/prometheus.yml <<EOF
global:
  scrape_interval: 15s
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: "prometheus"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["localhost:9090"]
  - job_name: "rabbitmq"
    static_configs:
      - targets:
          - "rabbitmq-0.rabbitmq-headless.$NS.svc.cluster.local:15692"
          - "rabbitmq-1.rabbitmq-headless.$NS.svc.cluster.local:15692"
          - "rabbitmq-2.rabbitmq-headless.$NS.svc.cluster.local:15692"
EOF
kubectl -n $NS create configmap prometheus-config \
  --from-file=prometheus.yml=/tmp/prometheus.yml \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl -n $NS apply -f -
input.description: Update prometheus-config with rabbitmq scrape job

kubectl -n $NS rollout restart deploy/prometheus
kubectl -n $NS rollout status deploy/prometheus --timeout=120s

input.command:

content.text: `rabbitmq_up` isn't in the default endpoint. Let me check all endpoints/metrics for it.

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-maximal-lifecycle-roll-af2b323e-cluster-a
echo "=== grep _up in /metrics ==="
kubectl -n $NS exec curl-test -- curl -s http://rabbitmq-0.rabbitmq-headless.$NS.svc.cluster.local:15692/metrics | grep -iE "_up[ {]" | head
echo "=== /metrics/per-object grep ==="
kubectl -n $NS exec curl-test -- curl -s "http://rabbitmq-0.rabbitmq-headless.$NS.svc.cluster.local:15692/metrics/per-object" | grep -iE "^rabbitmq_up" | head

content.text: Confirmed `rabbitmq_up` is not natively exposed. The intended approach: a Prometheus recording rule that materializes `rabbitmq_up` from `up{job="rabbitmq"}`. Let me add a rules file.

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-maximal-lifecycle-roll-af2b323e-cluster-a
cat > /tmp/prometheus.yml <<EOF
global:
  scrape_interval: 15s
  evaluation_interval: 15s
rule_files:
  - /etc/prometheus/rules.yml
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: "prometheus"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["localhost:9090"]
  - job_name: "rabbitmq"
    static_configs:
      - targets:
          - "rabbitmq-0.rabbitmq-headless.$NS.svc.cluster.local:15692"
          - "rabbitmq-1.rabbitmq-headless.$NS.svc.cluster.local:15692"
          - "rabbitmq-2.rabbitmq-headless.$NS.svc.cluster.local:15692"
EOF
cat > /tmp/rules.yml <<'EOF'
groups:
  - name: rabbitmq
    rules:
      - record: rabbitmq_up
        expr: up{job="rabbitmq"}
EOF
kubectl -n $NS create configmap prometheus-config \
  --from-file=prometheus.yml=/tmp/prometheus.yml \
  --from-file=rules.yml=/tmp/rules.yml \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl -n $NS apply -f -
kubectl -n $NS rollout restart deploy/prometheus
kubectl -n $NS rollout status deploy/prometheus --timeout=120s
input.description: Add recording rule for rabbitmq_up and restart

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-maximal-lifecycle-roll-af2b323e-cluster-a
# wait for scrape + rule eval
sleep 35
echo "=== rabbitmq targets ==="
kubectl -n $NS exec curl-test -- curl -s "http://prometheus.$NS.svc.cluster.local:8000/api/v1/targets" | python3 -c 'import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); ups=[t for t in d["data"]["activeTargets"] if t["labels"].get("job")=="rabbitmq" and t["health"]=="up"]; print("rabbitmq UP targets:",len(ups))'
echo "=== rabbitmq_up ==="
kubectl -n $NS exec curl-test -- curl -s "http://prometheus.$NS.svc.cluster.local:8000/api/v1/query?query=rabbitmq_up" | python3 -c 'import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print("samples:",len(d["data"]["result"])); [print(r["metric"].get("instance"), r["value"][1]) for r in d["data"]["result"]]'
echo "=== rabbitmq cluster health ==="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl cluster_status 2>/dev/null | grep -A4 "Running Nodes"
kubectl -n $NS get pods -l app=rabbitmq 2>/dev/null || kubectl -n $NS get pods | grep rabbitmq-
input.description: Verify targets, rabbitmq_up samples, cluster health
